name: Cross-Agent Consistency
description: Tests whether all agents follow the same memorized coding style when given the same task. With brain memory, all agents should produce structurally similar output matching the stored style preferences.

memories.title: TypeScript Style Guide
memories.body: All code must use TypeScript strict mode. Interfaces over types for object shapes. Enum for fixed sets. Zod for runtime validation. Barrel exports from index.ts files.
memories.content:
# TypeScript Style Guide

All code must use TypeScript strict mode. Interfaces over types for object shapes. Enum for fixed sets. Zod for runtime validation. Barrel exports from index.ts files.

## Rules
- Use `interface` for object shapes, not `type` aliases
- Use `enum` for fixed value sets
- Use Zod schemas for runtime validation of external input
- Every directory has an `index.ts` barrel export
- Prefer `readonly` properties where mutation isn't needed
- Use `unknown` over `any`
- Use `satisfies` operator for type narrowing
- Error types extend a base `AppError` class

memories.title: Naming Conventions
memories.body: PascalCase for interfaces and classes. camelCase for functions and variables. UPPER_SNAKE for constants. Files use kebab-case. Prefix interfaces with I only for React prop types (IProps).
memories.content:
# Naming Conventions

PascalCase for interfaces and classes. camelCase for functions and variables. UPPER_SNAKE for constants. Files use kebab-case.

## Examples
- Interface: `UserProfile`, `ApiResponse`
- Function: `getUserById`, `validateInput`
- Constant: `MAX_RETRIES`, `DEFAULT_TIMEOUT`
- File: `user-profile.ts`, `api-response.ts`

test.label: create-user-service
test.text: Create a TypeScript user service module at src/user-service.ts following the same patterns as the existing src/product-service.ts. It should have: a User interface, a UserRole enum, a Zod validation schema for user input, and a UserService class with async create, findById, findAll, and delete methods. Export everything from the module.
